mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy > 55% over 50 historical triggers.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving corporate earnings growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 58%, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series.

mechanism_narrative: A consecutive two-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side constraints, prompting immediate market repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive accuracy during periods of stable industrial output, or 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of decline episodes, invalidating the short-term supply shock transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 235.0 while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5 percent, real yields turn deeply negative, triggering inflation hedging flows and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive or neutral during high-inflation/low-rate episodes, or the threshold condition fails to separate positive from negative return regimes with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 60%, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and higher corporate revenues, generating immediate positive sentiment and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no significant correlation with 5-day forward returns, or the signal produces negative returns in > 55% of positive growth quarters over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable consumer prices signals healthy supply-side growth without inflationary trade-offs, reinforcing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails when inflation volatility exceeds 0.5% monthly, or 20-day forward returns turn negative in > 60% of stable-price industrial growth episodes.
